checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?
Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=309938000 (2009-12-26) rising to InventoryNet=387577000 (2010-12-25) while SalesRevenueNet fell from 2946440000 (FY2009) to 2689911000 (FY2010)
GrossProfit=1338592000 on SalesRevenueNet=2758569000 (FY2011, ~48.5% margin) vs GrossProfit=1438480000 on SalesRevenueNet=2715675000 (FY2012, ~53.0% margin) despite roughly flat revenue

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss quarterly FY2012: 86858000 (Q1), 185904000 (Q2), 140348000 (Q3), 129293000 (Q4) - substantial quarter-to-quarter swings tracking revenue seasonality (SalesRevenueNet 556597000, 718154000, 672376000, 768548000)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: ProductWarrantyAccrualClassifiedCurrent=87424000 (2009-12-26) declining to 49885000 (2010-12-25), 46773000 (2011-12-31), and 37301000 (2012-12-29) while SalesRevenueNet remained in the 2.7-2.9B range each year
AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=42409000 (2008-12-27) declining to 30596000 (2012-12-29) even as AccountsReceivableNetCurrent fluctuated between 600-870M over the period
evidence.location: 10-K FY2012, allowance for doubtful accounts note

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?
Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Financial series data spans FY2006 through Q1 2013 (e.g., SalesRevenueNet from 2006-12-31/2007-12-29 through 2012-12-30/2013-03-30) across multiple 10-K and 10-Q filings

top_signals: Warranty accrual as a % of revenue declined markedly from ~3.0% (FY2009: ProductWarrantyAccrualClassifiedCurrent=87424000 / SalesRevenueNet=2946440000) to ~1.4% (FY2012: ProductWarrantyAccrualClassifiedCurrent=37301000 / SalesRevenueNet=2715675000), a potential reserve-release earnings lever though could reflect genuine product quality improvement

Gross margin expanded sharply in FY2012 (GrossProfit=1438480000 / SalesRevenueNet=2715675000 ≈53.0%) versus FY2011 (~48.5%) despite roughly flat revenue, warranting scrutiny of cost allocation or mix-shift explanations

Goodwill grew ~31% in FY2011 (Goodwill=179475000 vs 136548000 in FY2010) faster than total assets (~12% growth), consistent with acquisition activity that should be checked for purchase-price-allocation reasonableness
Two 10-Q/A amendments (2009-09-01 and 2011-08-30) indicate at least two quarters were restated, a mild process-quality flag

Positive quality indicators: operating cash flow consistently exceeded net income every year (e.g., FY2012 OCF=684745000 > NI=542403000), and receivables grew slower than/in line with revenue, reducing overall concern
